rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors in binary expressions and invalid helper declarations.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes as per AADL annex rules.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with expression types using int, real, or bool.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid <error> type issues.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are properly scoped and referenced within the same annex or visible scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, correct the root cause or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
